dimensions.name: correctness
dimensions.description: Does the code correctly solve the task as specified? High = produces the right output for typical inputs and handles documented edge cases. Low = wrong logic, missing required behavior, or doesn't compile/run.

dimensions.name: conciseness
dimensions.description: Is the code minimal — no unnecessary lines, no over-engineering, no helper functions where an inline expression works? High = the shortest correct expression. Low = padded with unneeded boilerplate, defensive code for impossible errors, or excessive abstraction.

dimensions.name: no_fabricated_apis
dimensions.description: Does the code reference only real library functions? High = uses verified stdlib/well-known third-party APIs correctly. Low = invents non-existent functions (e.g., `email.is_valid()`, `os.read_json()`) or misuses real ones.

hard_gates.name: no_refusal
hard_gates.description: The model must attempt the task. Empty responses, refusals, or off-topic deflections fail this gate.

hard_gates.name: syntactically_valid
hard_gates.description: The code must be syntactically valid in the named language (parses without SyntaxError). Pseudocode, broken indentation, or unclosed brackets fail this gate.

hard_gates.name: no_unnecessary_comments
hard_gates.description: The code must NOT include comments for obvious operations (e.g., `# initialize result to 1`, `# return the result`). Only non-obvious WHY is acceptable. Verbose docstrings on trivial functions fail this gate.
